mnemonics: Model Views Controller: M(odel) V(iews) C(ontroller) - Make Very Clean applications by separating the Model, View, and Controller components.
keyPoints: The Model represents the application's data and business logic.
The View is responsible for displaying the data (the UI) to the user.
The Controller handles user input, interacts with the Model, and selects the View to display.
commonMistakes: Putting business logic in the View instead of the Model.
Allowing the Controller to perform data access operations instead of delegating them to the Model.
Creating tight coupling between the View and the Model, breaking the separation of concerns.
